annotations.label: Fluid mechanics

anchor.snippet: Fluid mechanics is the branch of physics concerned with the mechanics of fluids

annotations.note: Fluid mechanics as a physics discipline has no Mathlib formalization (grep for 'fluid' in Mathlib returns nothing).

annotations.label: Fluid statics vs fluid dynamics

anchor.snippet: It can be divided into fluid statics , the study of various fluids at rest

annotations.note: This taxonomic distinction between fluid subdisciplines has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Continuum mechanics

anchor.snippet: It is a branch of continuum mechanics , a subject which models matter without using the information that it is made out of atoms

annotations.note: Continuum mechanics as a modeling framework is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Computational fluid dynamics

anchor.snippet: A modern discipline, called computational fluid dynamics (CFD), is devoted to this approach.

annotations.note: CFD is a numerical/computational discipline not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fluid statics (hydrostatics)

anchor.snippet: Fluid statics or hydrostatics is the branch of fluid mechanics that studies fluids at rest.

annotations.note: No hydrostatics definitions exist in Mathlib (grep for 'hydrostat' returns nothing).

annotations.label: Fluid dynamics

anchor.snippet: Fluid dynamics is a subdiscipline of fluid mechanics that deals with fluid flow

annotations.note: Fluid dynamics is a physics discipline with no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mechanical definition of a fluid

anchor.snippet: In a mechanical view, a fluid is a substance that does not support shear stress

annotations.note: The continuum-mechanical notion of a fluid and shear stress is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Governing conservation assumptions

anchor.snippet: every fluid mechanical system is assumed to obey

annotations.note: Physical conservation assumptions (mass, momentum, energy) are not modeled in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conservation of mass over a control volume

anchor.snippet: the assumption that mass is conserved means that for any fixed control volume

annotations.note: Mathlib has the divergence theorem but no formalized mass-conservation/control-volume physics statement.

annotations.label: Continuum assumption

anchor.snippet: The continuum assumption is an idealization of continuum mechanics under which fluids can be treated as continuous

annotations.note: The continuum-mechanics modeling idealization is not formalized in Mathlib (its 'continuum' refers to cardinality).

annotations.label: Knudsen number

anchor.snippet: the Knudsen number , defined as the ratio of the molecular mean free path to the characteristic length scale

annotations.note: The Knudsen number is a physical dimensionless quantity absent from Mathlib (grep returns nothing).

annotations.label: Navier–Stokes equations

anchor.snippet: are differential equations that describe the force balance at a given point within a fluid

annotations.note: The Navier–Stokes equations are not formalized in Mathlib (grep for 'Navier' returns nothing).

annotations.label: Kinematic viscosity

anchor.snippet: parameterized by the kinematic viscosity

annotations.note: Kinematic viscosity as a fluid-mechanics parameter is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reynolds number

anchor.snippet: non-turbulent, steady flow in which the Reynolds number is small

annotations.note: The Reynolds number is a dimensionless physical quantity not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inviscid fluid

anchor.snippet: An inviscid fluid has no viscosity

annotations.note: Viscosity and inviscid fluids are physical notions not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No-slip condition

anchor.snippet: where the flow must match onto the no-slip condition at the solid

annotations.note: The no-slip boundary condition for viscous flow is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incompressible gas assumption

anchor.snippet: to assume that gas is incompressible —that is, the density of the gas does not change

annotations.note: Incompressibility of a fluid/gas is not formalized in Mathlib (grep for 'incompressible' returns nothing).

annotations.label: Newtonian fluid

anchor.snippet: A Newtonian fluid (named after Isaac Newton ) is defined to be a fluid whose shear stress is linearly proportional to the velocity gradient

annotations.note: The Newtonian fluid constitutive relation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Water as a Newtonian fluid

anchor.snippet: water is a Newtonian fluid, because it continues to display fluid properties

annotations.note: An empirical example about water has no place in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-Newtonian fluid

anchor.snippet: There are many types of non-Newtonian fluids, as they are defined to be something that fails to obey a particular property

annotations.note: Non-Newtonian fluids are a physics classification absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Viscosity as constant of proportionality

anchor.snippet: The constant of proportionality between the viscous stress tensor and the velocity gradient is known as the viscosity .

annotations.note: Viscosity as a physical coefficient relating stress and strain rate is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incompressible Newtonian fluid equation

anchor.snippet: A simple equation to describe incompressible Newtonian fluid behavior is

annotations.note: The incompressible Newtonian fluid momentum equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Viscous stress (incompressible, Cartesian)

anchor.snippet: If the fluid is incompressible the equation governing the viscous stress (in Cartesian coordinates ) is

annotations.note: The incompressible viscous stress tensor expression is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General viscous stress for Newtonian fluid

anchor.snippet: If the fluid is not incompressible the general form for the viscous stress in a Newtonian fluid is

annotations.note: The general compressible Newtonian viscous stress relation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bulk viscosity (second viscosity coefficient)

anchor.snippet: where [MATH] is the second viscosity coefficient (or bulk viscosity)

annotations.note: The bulk/second viscosity coefficient is a fluid-mechanics constitutive parameter not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ideal fluid

anchor.snippet: An ideal fluid is non-viscous and offers no resistance whatsoever to a shearing force.

annotations.note: The notion of an ideal (non-viscous) fluid is a physics concept absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler equation

anchor.snippet: The equation reduced in this form is called the Euler equation .

annotations.note: The Euler equations of fluid dynamics are not formalized in Mathlib (its 'Euler' results concern number theory/geometry).
